title: Hierarchical prose-to-source method matcher with fuzzy fallback
purpose: Matches prose methods to source methods using a 6-level fallback chain and provides quality metadata for each match or orphan.
module_context: Exports MatchQuality type and OverlayResult interface. Defines functions for building indices, performing matching steps, calculating fuzzy suggestions, and generating warnings.

steps.content: Replace all backslashes in the path with forward slashes.
Return the normalized path string.
Initialize maps for indexing by ID, file path, and body fingerprint.
Iterate through each source method row and populate the indices.
For each row, add it to the byId map using its ID as the key.
For each row, add it to the byFile map under its normalized file path.
For each row with a body fingerprint, add it to the byFingerprint map.
Return the populated indices object.
Compare the enclosing class of the source method row with that of the prose method.
Return true if both classes are null or if they match exactly.
Return false if the classes do not match.
Look up the prose method ID in the byId index.
Return the matching source method row if found, otherwise return null.
Iterate through each source method row in the file bucket.
Check if the row's class, name, and parameters match the prose method.
Return the first matching row if found, otherwise return null.
Iterate through each source method row in the file bucket.
Check if the row's class and name match the prose method.
Return the first matching row if found, otherwise return null.
Iterate through each source method row in the file bucket.
Check if the row's name matches the prose method's name.
Return the first matching row if found, otherwise return null.
Check if the prose method has a body fingerprint.
If no fingerprint, return null.
Iterate through each source method row in the file bucket.
Check if the row's fingerprint matches the prose method's fingerprint.
Return the first matching row if found, otherwise return null.

methods.name: step6FingerprintGlobal
methods.normalized_params: (idx: Indices, prose: ProseMethod, claimed: Set<string>): SourceMethodRow | null

steps.content: Check if the prose method has a body fingerprint.
If no fingerprint, return null.
Look up candidates with matching fingerprint in the global index.
If no candidates found, return null.
Iterate through candidates to find one not yet claimed.
Return the first unclaimed candidate if found, otherwise return null.
If strings are identical, return zero distance.
If one string is empty, return the length of the other.
Initialize dynamic programming arrays for computing edit distance.
Fill the dynamic programming table using character comparisons.
Return the computed edit distance between the strings.
Calculate the maximum length of the two strings.
If both strings are empty, return perfect similarity.
Compute the Levenshtein distance between the strings.
Normalize the distance by the maximum length to get similarity score.
Return the similarity score as a value between zero and one.

methods.name: fuzzySuggestions
methods.normalized_params: (proseFilePath: string, prose: ProseMethod, sourceMethods: SourceMethodRow[]): FuzzyMatch[]

steps.content: Normalize the prose file path and extract its directory.
Initialize an empty list of fuzzy matches.
Iterate through each source method row.
Check if the source method's directory matches the prose method's directory.
If directories match, calculate name similarity and fingerprint match.
Compute a combined score for the match.
Add the match to the candidates list if it meets the minimum score.
Sort candidates by score in descending order.
Return the top three candidates.

methods.name: warningFor
methods.normalized_params: (quality: MatchQuality, prose: ProseMethod, row: SourceMethodRow): string | undefined

steps.content: Evaluate the match quality to determine the appropriate warning message.
For parameter mismatch, describe the signature drift.
For class mismatch, describe the enclosing class drift.
For fuzzy rename, indicate a likely rename within the file.
For fuzzy move, indicate a likely move across files.
Return undefined for other quality types.
Build indices from the source methods for efficient lookup.
Initialize maps and arrays for tracking matches, orphans, and claimed IDs.
Iterate through each prose file and its methods.
For each prose method, attempt to match it using the 6-level fallback chain.
If a match is found, record it in the matches and attached prose maps.
If no match is found, add the method to orphans with fuzzy suggestions.
Return the final overlay result containing matches, orphans, and attached prose.
